stemHtml: The pilots of an aircraft receive a Traffic Advisory (TA) while en-route. The conflicting traffic is shown on the navigation display. Why should the pilots not respond based on the TA?

options.a: Making a rapid avoidance manoeuvre WITHOUT the guidance of a Resolution Advisory (RA) has a very high risk of upsetting the aircraft and should therefore be avoided.
options.b: The navigation display provides limited, partial information on the conflicting traffic's bearing and altitude which can be misinterpreted, and taking action based on this is hazardous.
options.c: The crew should wait for the reaction of the Air Traffic Controller. An early, rapid avoidance would make it impossible for the Air Traffic Controller to issue deconflicting instructions.
options.d: If the crew manoeuvre the aircraft, the TCAS system will stop monitoring the conflicting traffic, and if a collision risk remains, it would fail to issue a proper Resolution Advisory (RA).
